title: hieracium froelichianum subsp. laevipedunculum
intro: Hieracium froelichianum subsp. laevipedunculum, also known as Rough Hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) native to Europe. The medicinal properties of Rough Hawkweed include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its sesquiterpene lactones and polyacetylenes compounds. The health benefits of this plant include its use in treating skin conditions such as eczema, acne, and wounds, as well as its potential anti-cancer properties. Rough Hawkweed can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
